text:
Okapi BM25 - Wikipedia
In information retrieval, Okapi BM25 (BM25) is an abbreviation of best matching 25. It is used by search engines to estimate the relevance of documents to a given search query, based on the probabilistic retrieval framework developed in the 1970s and 1980s by Stephen E. Robertson, Karen Spärck Jones, and others.
While BM25 uses a bag of words approach, rather than the various recurrent neural network implementations which handle text documents as an ordered sequence, it can still be very effective.
